name: kimiflow
description: Calm context-aware Kimiflow launcher and active-session feature/fix loop for Codex with natural mode aliases, background handles, agentic readiness, clean-worktree, red/green, plan-blocker, code-review, memory, Obsidian, and Vault gates.

interface.shortDescription: Feature & bug-fix loop with full/grill/plan/build/quick/review/audit/fix modes, micro-grill and gates, plus project intelligence (codebase/architecture map), repo docs generation, and local findings
interface.longDescription: Kimiflow opens with a calm context-aware launcher when invoked without a concrete task: it shows one recommended next action, install/cache version status, compact project/memory/run health, and keeps internal maintenance in drilldowns. It then routes the user into natural mode aliases (`kimiflow full`, `grill`, `plan`, `build`, `quick`, `review`, `audit`, `fix`), project mapping, background handle collection, agentic readiness checks, memory/recall curation, Obsidian auto-detect/connect, Terminal-based Obsidian MCP setup, Vault health/auth checks, Vault prefetch/sync handoffs, findings, backlog resume, existing-feature checks, feature work, bug fixes, improvement analysis, or docs. `full` includes grill/spec, research, plan-gate, and a stop before build approval; `quick` stays lean but still asks or confirms a few intent questions before coding; `grill`, `plan`, `review`, and `audit` do not edit code. A project-local active-session contract keeps follow-up requests inside the current Kimiflow run until it is finished, parked, failed, or aborted, with run-local item tracking and stale-state revalidation before finish. Background Handles keep long read-only or draft-producing work visible for deep codebase analysis, docs generation, security/advisory review, and improvement scans; stale or candidate-only results must be collected and verified before use. The Agentic Readiness Layer checks local blockers before risky trust/apply handoffs, background-result trust, autonomous continuation, prepared-plan reuse, or worker fan-out that may apply changes, and can write bounded sanitized context packets plus a local audit trail without network calls. The Terminal wizard lets the user enter the Obsidian API key outside chat, writes host config, and keeps secrets out of .kimiflow/. It also runs a disciplined feature and bug-fix loop in Codex, and can build a local project intelligence map before coding. Normal write runs require a clean working tree before they start, fix runs keep red/green BUG-REPRO evidence, and local diagnostics can flag typecheck/lint/LSP-adjacent issues before review and commit. It maps the codebase, architecture, conventions, tests, flows, stale sections, docs, opt-in improvement/refactoring ideas, and bounded project memory under .kimiflow/project/. Normal coding runs use that map and memory recall to understand what already exists before planning, then continue through a mechanical plan-blocker gate, evidence-based verification, a focused code-review ensemble with orchestrator-verified findings, and the commit gate. Existing-feature checks review behavior, frontend/backend or command/API wiring, contracts, state/data, tests, and docs/security without editing code.
defaultPrompt: Open kimiflow and show project status
Run kimiflow full for this feature
Show or collect kimiflow background handles
Review this change with the kimiflow ensemble
Check an already-built feature with kimiflow
Map and understand this codebase with kimiflow
Refresh kimiflow project memory and recall
Open Obsidian MCP Terminal setup with kimiflow
Connect and check Obsidian memory health with kimiflow
Find architecture and refactoring opportunities with kimiflow
